title: Finland Has 188,000 Officially Counted Islands — and the Number Keeps Growing
description: Finland doesn't just have islands — it counts every single one. The Finnish Institute of Natural Resources maintains a verified inventory of 188,000 islands larger than 500 square meters, and the number rises as new surveys are completed. One of the most specific geographic facts in the world. #Finland #Geography #Islands #Trivia #Facts

segments.voiceover: Finland has 188,000 official islands — and someone's job is to count them.
segments.broll_cue: aerial drone shot of Finnish lake archipelago with forested islands

segments.voiceover: The Finnish Institute of Natural Resources tracks every island larger than 500 square meters. That's a legal threshold Finland wrote into its own surveys. The count goes up whenever new mapping is done.
segments.broll_cue: topographic map of Finland showing lake district with island clusters marked

segments.voiceover: No other country maintains a verified island inventory like this. Finland didn't just find the islands — it defined what an island is.
segments.broll_cue: close-up of Finnish survey map or satellite image of Saimaa lake archipelago

segments.voiceover: Follow for one fact a day.
segments.broll_cue: static aerial shot of single small forested Finnish island surrounded by water

fact_check_flags: SUPERLATIVE: 'No other country maintains a verified island inventory like this' — verify this claim; Indonesia, Norway, and Canada also have large island counts and may have formal inventories
NUMBER: 188,000 islands — confirm this is the current figure from the Finnish Institute of Natural Resources (Luke); number may have been updated since source was last checked
THRESHOLD: 500 square meters as the legal/official minimum size threshold for island classification — verify this is the exact Finnish statutory or survey definition, not an approximation

INSTITUTION: 'Finnish Institute of Natural Resources' — confirm this is the correct English name for Luke (Luonnonvarakeskus) and that they are the body responsible for the island inventory, not the National Land Survey of Finland (Maanmittauslaitos)
CLAIM: 'The count goes up whenever new mapping is done' — verify this is accurate; confirm the inventory is actively updated and not a fixed historical survey
IMPLICIT SUPERLATIVE: Script implies Finland has the most counted/tracked islands in the world — verify or soften if this cannot be sourced

source_idea.title: Finland Has 188,000 Islands (and Counts Them)
source_idea.hook: Norway has the fjords. Sweden has the lakes. But Finland has so many islands it built a whole system to keep track of them—and the number keeps growing.
source_idea.fact_summary: Finland officially counts 188,000 islands larger than 500 square meters, defined by their own legal threshold. The count increases whenever surveys map new islands, making it the only country that actively maintains a verified island inventory.
source_idea.source_hint: Wikipedia: List of islands of Finland; Finnish Institute of Natural Resources
